results.title: Roderick Cassar handed more jail time for domestic violence incidents before wife's murder
results.description: Roderick Cassar, currently serving a 40-year prison sentence for the murder of his wife, has received two additional jail terms linked to domestic violence incidents that occurred before her murder. Cassar has now been sentenced to three months in prison for holding a knife to his wife's throat on Mother's Day in 2022. He was also handed an eight-month sentence for a separate incident on 14 July, 2022. In the second incident, Cassar was in hospital, as his wife Bernice had brought their children to visit him, but an argument broke out during which he threatened to kill her. Last November, Cassar was handed a 40-year prison sentence after admitting to the murder of his estranged wife, Bernice Cilia. In 2022, he shot his wife in the Corradino area as she was driving to work. The killing became the first case ever prosecuted under Malta's aggravated offence of wilful femicide. He was arrested following a 17-hour standoff at his home in Qrendi. Court testimony and public outrage in the aftermath of the murder highlighted systemic failings in the protection of domestic violence victims. Bernice had filed several police reports in the months prior to her death, including one the day before she was killed, reporting breaches of a protection order and threats to her life. A subsequent inquiry led by retired judge Geoffrey Valenzia found the state had failed to protect her, citing resource shortages and poor risk-assessment procedures. The inquiry recommended urgent reforms, including a triage system for domestic violence reports and new protocols to assess high-risk cases, after the DASH tool classified Bernice's situation as "medium risk."

results.title: Indian cancer patients battle shortage of key drugs as platinum costs surge

results.title: Men’s mental health month 2026: When ‘I’m fine’ isn’t the whole story, 6 conversations experts recommend
results.description: Despite growing mental health awareness, many men still struggle in silence, often conditioned to hide vulnerability. This Men's Mental Health Month, experts emphasize that life-changing conversations can start with simple, genuine check-ins. Asking about coping mechanisms, what's weighing on their minds, or if expectations are overwhelming can open crucial dialogues, fostering support and encouraging men to seek help when needed.

results.title: Rubio notified as 'Sleepy Don' Trump mocked for appearing to sleep at NBA Finals
results.description: President Donald Trump made a high-profile appearance at Game 3 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden on Monday — and video quickly spread appearing to show him nodding off courtside, prompting a California congressman to loop in the Secretary of State. Rep. Ted Lieu tagged Marco Rubio directly, writing simply: "cc: @SecRubio" — and attaching the viral clip. That's because, at a recent grilling before lawmakers, Rubio denied ever seeing the president sleeping in public . Lieu wasn't the only one to mark the occasion. Mockery was spreading quickly. Republicans Against Trump delivered the most pointed line: "WOW. Trump fell asleep at MSG during Game 3 of the NBA Finals. They don't call him Sleepy Don for nothing." Josh Burwick, a political commentator, went with a direct riff on Trump's military title: "Donald Trump the commander in sleep falls asleep at the NBA finals game!" Amy Siskind, a political activist and author, connected Trump's apparent nap to his insistence to attend the major sporting event in person rather than watch from home. "OMG, after ALL THAT, Trump is sleeping at the Knicks game!" she wrote. "Couldn't he have saved everyone the trouble and 'watch it on television?'" MeidasTouch, the liberal political media outlet, offered a more charitable read: "Trump takes a long blink during the NBA Finals." Legion Hoops, a basketball media account, kept it simple: "Donald Trump looks like he's falling asleep during Game 3 of the Finals." Call to Activism's original clip of Trump closing his eyes drew more than 11 million views in roughly eight hours.

Majhi said the naming has hurt the religious sentiments of millions of devotees of Mahaprabhu Shree Jagannath across Odisha [...] The post Odisha CM Writes to West Bengal CM to Drop “Dham” from Digha Jagannath Temple Name appeared first on Pragativadi I Latest Odisha News in English I Breaking News .

results.title: Five Arrested In Oman For Illegal Entry, Drug Smuggling Attempt
results.description: The Dhofar Governorate Police Command, in cooperation with the Coast Guard Police, has arrested five individuals who were attempting to enter the Sultanate of Oman illegally. The suspects were apprehended while trying to smuggle quantities of narcotic substances into the country aboard a fishing boat with the intent to traffic the drugs. Authorities confirmed that legal procedures are being completed against the accused. The Royal Oman Police reiterated its commitment to combating illegal infiltration and drug trafficking, emphasising ongoing efforts to safeguard the country’s borders and maintain public security. results.title: DU Student Arrested For Supplying Hydroponic Marijuana To College Students
results.description: A 22-year-old Delhi University student was arrested in north Delhi with 195 grams of hydroponic marijuana, valued at Rs 20 lakh. He allegedly supplied the drug, known as OG, to college students to fund a lavish lifestyle. Police are investigating the supply chain.
